title: alnus incana
intro: Alnus incana, also known as the grey alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Alnus incana lie in its bark, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain. The health benefits of the grey alder include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various conditions such as wounds, fever, and rheumatism. Alnus incana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
